title: Australia, New Zealand, and Pacific: Analysis, Research, & Events | CSIS
text:
Australia, New Zealand, and Pacific

Long-standing alliances and partnerships with Australia, New Zealand, and the Pacific Islands are an essential component of U.S. national security. CSIS fosters policy discussions on the future of the U.S.-Australia alliance, as well as deepening economic and technological exchange with Australia, advancing AUKUS, elevating cooperation with New Zealand, and bolstering U.S. engagement in the Pacific Islands amid rising strategic competition in the Indo-Pacific.

Image

Pacific Airwaves – New Leadership, New Currents

Andreyka and Monica discuss Prime Minister Matthew Wale's foreign policy, ocean governance, and other regional updates. This episode was recorded on June 29, 2026.

Was This World Cup More Political? | State of Play

Please join CSIS experts to assess the geopolitical dimensions of this year's World Cup in a live episode of State of Play. 

Event

— July 16, 2026

Navigating Strategic Competition: Senator Tammy Duckworth on the Future of U.S. Indo-Pacific Engagement

A Conversation with Senator Tammy Duckworth spotlights Senator Tammy Duckworth and her recent travel to the Indo-Pacific and Congressional perspectives on U.S. strategy in the region.

Event

— July 16, 2026

What the Iran War Revealed About U.S. Power | The High Top

How has the U.S.-Iran war changed the way the world views American power? Jon Alterman and Charles Edel discuss shifting views among allies and the Global South, the fraying post-1945 order, and the challenge of building a more durable allied strategy.

Event

— July 14, 2026

Sixteenth Annual South China Sea Conference

This full-day conference will feature keynote addresses and in-depth panel discussions on recent developments in disputed waters and the importance of the 10-year anniversary of the landmark South China Sea arbitration.

Event

— July 7, 2026

Reengaging the Solomon Islands: A Strategic Opportunity for the United States

Matthew Wale’s rise to power offers Washington its best opportunity in years to rebuild ties with the Solomon Islands, but seizing the moment will require more than diplomatic rhetoric.

Commentary
by

John Augé
and
Kathryn Paik

— June 5, 2026

The Quad Convenes: Priorities, Partnerships and the Indo-Pacific

The event will feature a panel discussion on the Quad with distinguished participants moderated by Senior Adviser and Chair on India and Emerging Asia Economics, Richard M. Rossow. Speakers include Luke Collin, Principal, The Asia Group, Dr. Nishank Motwani, Vice President at Levenhall.

Event

— May 28, 2026

Security and the Pacific Islands: Insights from Young Pacific Leaders Report Launch

Shaping Regional Security: Insights from Young Pacific Leaders spotlights Pacific voices on climate, maritime, and cyber challenges. Join experts and emerging leaders to explore how the U.S. and partners can strengthen regional security and cooperation.

Event

— May 26, 2026
